title: branch — HTTP routes
description: Tenant-facing branch CRUD + lifecycle transitions + hours + is-open probe + platform-admin cross-tenant read.

groups.name: tenant-branches
groups.description: Tenant-plane branch CRUD. Every scope-suffixed permission is resolved by ScopeSuffixResolver at query build time. Note: this group does NOT auto-apply `branch.resolve_active` because the branch resource is what these routes manipulate.

routes.description: filter[organization_id], filter[region_id], filter[status], filter[has_amenities], filter[created_after], sort[]=sort_order, include=organization,region,facilities_count,active_teams_count,default_default

Body: { organization_id, region_id, name, slug?, code?, description?, logo_url?, address?, coordinates?, phone?, email?, website_url?, opening_hours?, capacity?, amenities?, is_default?, sort_order? }. Consumes 1 `branch_slot` entitlement.

Body: any of { name, slug, code, description, logo_url, phone, email, website_url, sort_order }. Address / coordinates / opening_hours / capacity / amenities go via the specific PATCH sub-routes below OR pass through this route when the caller carries the corresponding fine-grained permission. `organization_id` / `region_id` / `tenant_id` writes are refused (immutable).
Always returns 403 `BRANCH_PUBLIC_DELETE_FORBIDDEN` with guidance to use `POST /{branch}/close`. Kept in the routes list so callers get the guided response rather than a bare 405.
Body: full structured-address blob. Server-side re-computes the `formatted` field per branch.region.timezone locale.
Body: { lat, lng, accuracy_m?, source? }. `source` defaults to 'manual' — auto-geocoding writes flow through the job, not this endpoint.
Body: full opening_hours blob (7 day keys + exceptions). Refuses multi-period days when tenant lacks `branch_multi_period_hours` entitlement.
Body: { capacity: int | null }. Null clears the cap. Fires BranchCapacityChanged.
Idempotent. Auto-demotes the previous default row for the same (tenant, organization). Fires BranchDefaultChanged.
Body: { reason?, resume_scheduled_for? }. Refuses when status !== 'active'.
Body: { reason, closed_date?, successor_branch_id? }. Refuses when active downstream rows reference the branch (BRANCH_IN_USE, 409).
Refuses when a successor branch was created under the same (tenant, organization) post-close (BRANCH_SUCCESSOR_EXISTS, 409).

Dispatches GeocodeAddressJob for re-resolution. Returns 402 `BRANCH_GEOCODING_UNAVAILABLE` when tenant lacks `branch_geocoding` entitlement. Idempotent — the job de-duplicates on (branch_id, address_hash).
Returns opening_hours + current-status metadata: is_open_now, next_open_at, next_close_at, computed in `branch.region.timezone` (caller's tz optionally overrides via ?tz= param).
Boolean + reason. Response: { is_open: bool, reason: 'active'|'paused'|'closed'|'outside_hours'|'exception_day', next_transition_at: ISO-8601 }.

groups.name: platform-branches
groups.description: Cross-tenant read for support tooling. Writes stay on the tenant surface apart from the emergency force-close.

routes.description: Cross-tenant filterable list. filter[tenant_id], filter[organization_id], filter[region_id], filter[status], filter[country_code].

Compliance-forced closure. Bypasses the dependency-check kill switch. Body: { reason, ticket_url }. Fires an alert on every use + writes an audit row with severity=warn. Never used for the ordinary case — the tenant `close` endpoint handles that.
